'''Kai O'Donnell''' (born 21 February 1999) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Qld Cowboys]] in the [[NRL]].

==Background==
Born in [[Brisbane, Queensland]], O'Donnell played his junior rugby league for the Proserpine Brahmans and attended [[Proserpine State High School]] before being signed by the [[Gold Coast Titans]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qt.com.au/news/talent-in-cowboys-academy/3036968/|title=Talent in Cowboys academy|date=1 June 2016|website=Queensland Times}}</ref> While on the [[Gold Coast, Queensland|Gold Coast]], he attended [[Palm Beach Currumbin State High School]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://18thman.com/2017/07/07/2017-assrl-18-years-championship-guide/|title=2017 ASSRL 18 Years Championship guide|date=7 July 2017|website=18th Man}}</ref>

==Playing career==
In 2015, O'Donnell played for the [[Mackay Cutters]] [[Cyril Connell Cup]] side.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.whitsundaytimes.com.au/news/five-junior-brahmans-picked-in-cutters-squads/2533242/|title=Five junior Brahmans picked in Cutters' squads|date=6 February 2015|website=Whitsunday Times}}</ref> In 2017, he played for the [[Burleigh Bears]] [[Mal Meninga Cup]] side and moved up to their [[Hastings Deering Colts]] side in 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2017/03/29/bears-post-opening-mm-cup-win/|title=Bears post opening MM Cup win|date=29 March 2017|website=QRL}}</ref> Later in 2018, O'Donnell made his debut for the Bears' [[Queensland Cup]] side.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sunshinecoastdaily.com.au/news/super-cup-debut-for-our-kai/3477147/|title=Super Cup debut for our Kai|date=28 July 2018|website=Whitsunday Times}}</ref>

In February 2019, O'Donnell came off the bench in the [[Gold Coast Titans]] pre-season trial loss to the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2019/02/21/watch-trial-livestream-this-saturday/|title=Watch trial live stream this Saturday|date=21 February 2019|website=Gold Coast Titans}}</ref> In April 2019, he made a mid-season move to the [[Canberra Raiders]], joining their [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] side and starting at {{rlp|sr}} in their Grand Final loss to the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://18thman.com/jersey-flegg-cup/2019-2/finals-results/canberra-v-south-sydney/|title=Canberra v South Sydney|website=18th Man}}</ref>

===2020===
In February, O'Donnell was a member of the Raiders' [[NRL Nines]] squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.canberratimes.com.au/story/6624484/whitehead-to-captain-raiders-at-nrl-nines/|title=Whitehead to captain Raiders at NRL Nines|date=11 February 2020|website=Canberra Times}}</ref>

In round 9 of the [[2020 NRL season]], O'Donnell made his NRL debut for Canberra against the [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2020/07/07/raider-365---kai-odonnell/|title=Raider 365 - Kai O'Donnell|date=7 July 2020|website=Canberra Raiders}}</ref>

In round 20, he scored his first try in the top grade during a 38-28 victory over Cronulla-Sutherland at [[Kogarah Oval]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-09-26/nrl-parramatta-beats-wests-tigers-as-panthers-raiders-post-wins/12707470/|title=Parramatta beats Wests Tigers to claim third spot on NRL ladder, Panthers and Raiders score wins|website=www.abc.net.au}}</ref>

===2022===
On 8 April, O'Donnell signed a contract to join [[RFL Championship]] side [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/news/20057039.leigh-centurions-sign-nrl-forward-kai-odonnell/#:~:text=LEIGH%20Centurions%20have%20signed%20NRL,Ashington%20in%20the%20North%2DEast./|title=Leigh Centurions sign NRL forward Kai O'Donnell|website=www.leighjournal.co.uk}}</ref>

===2023===
In the 2023 Challenge Cup quarter-final, O'Donnell was sent off for a spear tackle in Leigh's 34-14 victory over [[York Knights|York RLFC]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/york-knights-14-leigh-leopards-34-plucky-underdogs-give-super-league-high-flyers-huge-scare-on-proud-night-4187295/|title=York Knights 14 Leigh Leopards 34: Plucky underdogs give Super League high-flyers huge scare on proud night|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>
On 20 June, O'Donnell was given a six-game ban by the RFL over the incident.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65953290|title=Josh Griffin: Hull FC centre gets seven-game ban after red card in Challenge Cup loss|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
O'Donnell played 22 games for Leigh in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fifth on the table and qualified for the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/leigh-leopards-2023-in-review/#:~:text=Overall%20playing%20record%3A,For%20694%2C%20Against%20580./|title=Leigh Leopards 2023 season in review|website=www.leighleopards.co.uk}}</ref>

===2024===
He was highly praised by head coach [[Adrian Lam]] after scoring four tries in [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh's]] 42-12 victory over [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League |date=12 Aug 2024|title= Kai O'Donnell makes Leigh Leopards history as coach makes 'best in Super League' call|url= https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/kai-odonnell-makes-leigh-leopards-history-adrian-lam}}</ref>
O'Donnell played 27 games for Leigh in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish fifth on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live-blog/12204/13227859/super-league-semi-finals-live-wigan-face-leigh-for-grand-final-spot/|title=Super League semi-finals: Wigan Warriors cruise past Leigh Leopards- live commentary and video clips|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

===2025===
O'Donnell played 18 games for North Queensland in the [[2025 NRL season]] as the club finished 12th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2025-the-mole-season-review-north-queensland-cowboys-jason-taumalolo-deal-20250911-p5mugv.html/|title=The Mole's season review: 'Leaderless' Cowboys prove $10m gamble on Jason Taumalolo was a flop|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>
